nativeRange.habitat: Slow-moving creeks and tributaries in floodplain forests; typically over soft sandy substrate with leaf litter and submerged wood; cooler subtropical conditions compared to most Amazonian Apistogramma

decorPreferences: dim lighting or floating plants for shade

diet.notes: Primarily insectivorous and benthic invertebrate feeder in the wild. In the aquarium, tends to accept frozen or live daphnia, baby brine shrimp, bloodworms, and cyclops readily. Most individuals have been reported to accept sinking micro-pellets and high-quality flake foods with conditioning, though live and frozen foods are broadly recommended for best health and coloration.

avoidWith: shrimp (especially when breeding female is present)

summary: A cool-tolerant Apistogramma from the Paraná basin; males display vivid blue iridescence ('Opal' form) or golden ('Yellow' form). Suitable for unheated indoor tanks. Intermediate care; rewarding cave-spawner with strong female brood care.
careNotes:
A. borellii is notable among dwarf cichlids for its tolerance of cooler water — it is native to subtropical Paraguay, Bolivia, and Argentina rather than the warm Amazonian lowlands typical of most Apistogramma species. This means it can generally be maintained in unheated indoor tanks at room temperature in temperate climates, an unusual trait for the genus.

Two widely traded color forms exist: the 'Opal' form (males displaying extensive blue-violet iridescence on the body and fins) and the 'Yellow' form (males with golden-yellow body and fins). Both are the same species and share identical care requirements.

Best kept as a harem group — one male with two or three females — in a tank of 75 L or more with multiple cave spawning sites, dense planting, and hardscape to break lines of sight between females. The species is naturally haremic; males are noticeably larger and more colorful, while females become vivid yellow-orange when guarding eggs or fry and can be quite aggressive toward both the male and other tank inhabitants during broodcare.

Dither fish (small, active mid-water species such as tetras or pencilfish) are commonly recommended to reduce shyness and distribute female aggression. Avoid housing with other Apistogramma species due to territorial overlap and potential hybridization.

Water quality should be kept stable with minimal nitrate accumulation (ideally below 20–40 ppm); regular partial water changes are advised. Strong flow or powerful filtration is generally not appropriate — gentle filtration matching the species' slow-water habitat is preferable.

Sources showed minor disagreement on temperature upper limit: SeriouslyFish gives 20–26°C, Aquarium Co-Op's general guide suggests 27–28°C (80–82°F), and FishBase data indicates 24–25°C. The 26°C upper bound is used here as it aligns with the SeriouslyFish specialist profile and is consistent with the species' subtropical cool-tolerant reputation. At the cooler end (20–22°C), the species has been reported to thrive and breed — a useful trait for fishkeepers without aquarium heaters.

Sister species in catalog: Apistogramma agassizii (fw-048) and Apistogramma cacatuoides (fw-047) both prefer warmer, more purely Amazonian conditions.
breedingNotes: Cave spawner with strong maternal brood care. Females typically deposit 50–70 eggs on the ceiling or interior of a cave, coconut hut, or similar shelter. The female guards and tends the eggs; incubation takes approximately 48–72 hours depending on temperature. The female continues to guard free-swimming fry aggressively and may require the male's removal from smaller tanks during broodcare. Breeding is generally considered moderately achievable in captivity; soft, slightly acidic conditions (pH 6.0–6.8, gH 2–8) and caves are the primary triggers. Females may spawn multiple times per season.

primary.name: SeriouslyFish
primary.notes: Primary husbandry reference. Swapped to primary role after Aquarium Co-Op's borellii-specific page returned 404; the Aquarium Co-Op general Apistogramma guide was used as secondary. SeriouslyFish is the most detailed per-species source for borellii.

additional.name: Aquarium Co-Op
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (males ~3–3.5 in / 7.6–8.9 cm), water temperature (80–82°F / 27–28°C), tank size (minimum 20 gal / 76 L), diet, and temperament. General Apistogramma guide with borellii section. Temperature upper limit slightly higher than SeriouslyFish; 26°C ceiling retained per SeriouslyFish specialist profile.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Cichlidae, Cichliformes), native range (Paraguay River basin and lower Paraná River in Argentina), water parameters (24–25°C, pH 6.0–8.0, dH 5–19), max standard length (3.9 cm SL for males), and egg count (50–70). SL is standard length — total length with fins is approximately 6–8 cm, consistent with SeriouslyFish's 6.5 cm figure.

fish.breedingNotes: Cave spawner; female guards eggs and fry aggressively. Breeds in captivity with appropriate conditions (caves, soft slightly-acidic water). Male may need temporary removal in smaller tanks during female broodcare.
